Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents an individual item available for sale in the Super 9+ POS system, including inventory details and pricing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

name.description: The name of the product (e.g., Amul Milk).

description.description: A brief description of the product.

barcode.description: The barcode associated with the product, used for scanning.

imageUrl.description: URL to the product's image, used for quick-tap icons.

sellingPrice.description: The price at which the product is sold to customers.

costPrice.description: The cost price of the product, used for profit calculation.

stockQuantity.description: The current quantity of the product in stock. If not provided, inventory for this item is considered untracked.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

isTopSeller.description: Indicates if the product is among the top 20 most-sold items, for quick-tap access.

isActive.description: Indicates whether the product is currently active and available for sale.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the product's information.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Organizes products into logical groups for better management and search functionality.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., Dairy, Beverages).

description.description: A brief description of the category.

Customer.title: Customer
Customer.description: Stores information about a customer, used for loyalty programs, personalized offers, and purchase history tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Customer entity.

phoneNumber.description: The customer's phone number, used for identification and communication. This should be unique.

name.description: The full name of the customer.

email.description: The customer's email address for communication.

points.description: Loyalty points accumulated by the customer.

totalSpent.description: The total monetary amount the customer has spent.

lastVisitDate.description: The timestamp of the customer's last purchase or interaction. Used for 'We miss you' coupon logic.

isFlaggedForCoupon.description: Flag indicating if the customer is eligible for a 'We miss you' WhatsApp coupon.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the customer profile was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the customer's information.

Coupon.title: Coupon
Coupon.description: Defines discount coupons that can be applied to purchases, including their type, value, and validity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Coupon entity.

code.description: The unique code that customers use to redeem the coupon.

type.description: The type of discount offered (e.g., 'flat' for a fixed amount, 'percent' for a percentage off).

value.description: The value of the coupon (e.g., 50 for ₹50 off, or 10 for 10% off).

minBillAmount.description: The minimum total bill amount required for the coupon to be applicable.

maxDiscountAmount.description: The maximum discount amount that can be applied if the coupon is a percentage type.

expiryDate.description: The date and time when the coupon expires.

usageCount.description: The number of times this specific coupon code has been used.

maxUsagePerCustomer.description: The maximum number of times a single customer can use this coupon.

applicableProductIds.description: An array of Product IDs to which this coupon can be applied. If empty, it applies to all products. (Relationship: Product N:N Coupon)

applicableCategoryIds.description: An array of Category IDs to which this coupon can be applied. If empty, it applies to all categories. (Relationship: Category N:N Coupon)

isActive.description: Indicates whether the coupon is currently active and can be redeemed.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the coupon was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the coupon's information.

PricingRule.title: PricingRule
PricingRule.description: Defines dynamic pricing rules such as Happy Hour discounts or bulk purchase offers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PricingRule entity.

name.description: A descriptive name for the pricing rule (e.g., Happy Hour Drinks, Buy 3 Get 10% Off Milk).

discountPercentage.description: The percentage discount to apply (e.g., 10 for 10%).

discountFlatAmount.description: A fixed monetary amount to discount.

minQuantity.description: The minimum quantity of an item required for the bulk discount to apply.

minBillAmount.description: The minimum total bill amount for the rule to be applied.

startTime.description: The start date and time when the pricing rule becomes active.

endTime.description: The end date and time when the pricing rule expires.

applicableProductIds.description: An array of Product IDs to which this pricing rule applies. If empty, it applies to all products. (Relationship: Product N:N PricingRule)

applicableCategoryIds.description: An array of Category IDs to which this pricing rule applies. If empty, it applies to all categories. (Relationship: Category N:N PricingRule)

isActive.description: Indicates whether the pricing rule is currently active and applied automatically.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the pricing rule was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last update to the pricing rule's information.

Purchase.title: Purchase
Purchase.description: Records a single customer transaction or sale, including all items purchased, payment details, and applied discounts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Purchase entity.

customerId.description: Reference to the Customer who made the purchase. Optional if the customer is not identified. (Relationship: Customer 1:N Purchase)

staffId.description: Reference to the Staff member who processed the sale. (Relationship: Staff 1:N Purchase)

timestamp.description: The exact date and time when the purchase was made. Crucial for offline sync and reporting.

totalAmount.description: The final total amount of the purchase after all discounts.

subtotalAmount.description: The total amount of the purchase before any discounts were applied.

discountAmount.description: The total monetary amount discounted from the purchase.

items.description: An array of objects, each representing a product bought in this purchase.

couponCodeApplied.description: The code of the coupon applied to this purchase, if any. (Relationship: Coupon 1:N Purchase)

isOfflineSale.description: Indicates if this sale was initially recorded offline and awaiting server synchronization.

isCreditSale.description: Indicates if the sale was made on credit (Udhaar), triggering specific printing logic.

receiptLink.description: A URI link to the digital receipt sent via WhatsApp.

customerReviewLink.description: A URI link for the customer to leave a review, included in the WhatsApp receipt.

adminOverrideApplied.description: Indicates if an admin override was used to approve a discount on this purchase.

Staff.title: Staff
Staff.description: Manages staff accounts, roles, and permissions within the POS system, particularly for administrative overrides.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Staff entity, typically matching the external authentication system's user ID.

name.description: The name of the staff member.

role.description: The role of the staff member (e.g., 'Staff', 'Admin').

isActive.description: Indicates whether the staff account is currently active.

definition.description: Stores individual product details, including pricing (sellingPrice, costPrice) and optional inventory (stockQuantity). Supports fuzzy search and quick-tap icons. Includes denormalized 'isTopSeller' for UI prioritization.
Organizes products into logical groups for management and search. Globally readable by authenticated staff, writeable by admins.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: Unique identifier for a product category.

definition.description: Stores customer loyalty and purchase history. Includes 'isFlaggedForCoupon' for automated 'We miss you' offers. Globally accessible by authorized staff, writeable by admins/authorized staff.
Defines discount coupons for application during purchases. Globally readable by authenticated staff, writeable by admins.
Defines dynamic pricing rules (e.g., Happy Hour, Bulk Discounts). Globally readable by authenticated staff, writeable by admins.

params.name: ruleId
params.description: Unique identifier for a pricing rule.

definition.description: Records individual customer transactions. Includes 'staffId' (denormalized for authorization independence), 'isOfflineSale' for sync, and 'adminOverrideApplied' for security. Purchases are immutable after creation.

params.name: purchaseId
params.description: Unique identifier for a purchase transaction.

definition.description: Stores staff profiles, where 'id' matches Firebase Authentication UID. Allows self-read and admin read/write for all staff. Role-based access for security rules is handled by '/adminRoles/{staffId}' for authorization independence.

params.name: staffId
params.description: Unique identifier for a staff member, matching their Firebase Auth UID.

definition.description: CRITICAL for Authorization Independence. This collection's sole purpose is to indicate admin status. The presence of a document where 'staffId' matches 'request.auth.uid' grants admin privileges in security rules (using 'exists()').

params.name: staffId
params.description: Unique identifier for an admin staff member, matching their Firebase Auth UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The primary strategy employed is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** and **Structural Segregation**. All authorization logic avoids `get()` calls in security rules.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & QAPs:**

1.  **Admin Roles (`/adminRoles/{staffId}`):** To ensure authorization independence for administrative privileges, a dedicated collection `/adminRoles` is used. An authenticated user's `request.auth.uid` being present as a document ID in this collection directly grants admin access. Security rules will use `exists(/adminRoles/$(request.auth.uid))` for efficient and atomic role checks, completely eliminating `get()` calls. This directly supports QAPs as admin access is determined by document existence, not by filtering data within a document.

2.  **Staff-Specific Data (`/staff/{staffId}` and `/purchases/{purchaseId}`):**
    *   `staffId` in the path `/staff/{staffId}` directly ties a staff member to their profile, allowing simple self-read rules (`request.auth.uid == staffId`).
    *   For `Purchase` records, the `staffId` of the creating staff is denormalized and stored directly within each `Purchase` document. This allows security rules to check `resource.data.staffId == request.auth.uid` for individual staff access to their own sales, or `exists(/adminRoles/$(request.auth.uid))` for global admin access. This denormalization ensures that access to a purchase document does not depend on fetching any parent document, making operations atomic and rules debuggable.

3.  **Store-wide Data (`/products`, `/categories`, `/customers`, `/coupons`, `/pricingRules`):** These collections represent data that is globally accessible (read) by *any* authenticated staff member, and writable only by administrators. This adheres to **Structural Segregation** by grouping data with homogeneous security postures. Since all authenticated staff can read these documents, `list` operations are inherently secure and do not require rules to act as filters (QAPs), as any document returned is permissible.

**User Request Integration:**
*   **Inventory Optional:** The `stockQuantity` field in `Product` is designed to be optional. If not present or null, inventory tracking for that item is effectively disabled, per user request.
*   **Add Items/Prices in Search Bar:** The `Product` collection with `name`, `sellingPrice`, `costPrice`, and `barcode` fields supports robust search functionality and pricing management. The UI would query this collection.
*   **Sync Everything:** The `Purchase` documents include an `isOfflineSale` flag, enabling robust offline-first synchronization logic handled by Cloud Functions upon reconnection, leveraging Firestore's `enablePersistence(true)` feature for client-side resilience.

Overall, the structure is designed to minimize complexity in security rules, support efficient queries, and provide a clear separation of concerns based on access patterns.
